request.question: Isaac Durnford had a role on the Canadian series directed by whom?

title: Hagan Beggs
Tatiana Maslany
Roger Cross
Jack Christie
Cassinian
Daniel Gillies
Dino Dan
Erika Tham
Lara Jean Chorostecki
Isaac Durnford
sentences: Hagan Beggs (March 19, 1937 – September 16, 2016) was a Northern Irish-born Canadian actor.
He was best known for starring as Dr. George Dunbar on the Canadian television series, "Danger Bay", which aired on CBC Television and The Disney Channel from 1985 to 1990.
Beggs also co-starred as Liam Gleeson on the Canadian series, "Bordertown", from 1989 to 1991.
His other television roles included a recurring role as Lt. Hansen during of "" from 1966 to 1967.

Tatiana Gabriele Maslany (born September 22, 1985) is a Canadian actress best known for playing multiple roles in the science fiction thriller TV series "Orphan Black" (2013–2017), which aired on Space in Canada and BBC America in the US.

For her performances in "Orphan Black", Maslany won the Primetime Emmy Award (2016), the TCA Award (2013), two Critics' Choice Television Awards (2013 and 2014), and four Canadian Screen Awards (2014–17), in addition to receiving a Golden Globe Award and Screen Actors Guild Award nomination.
Maslany became the first Canadian actor from a Canadian series to win an Emmy Award in a key dramatic category.
Roger Cross (born October 19, 1969) is a Jamaican-born Canadian actor who has made numerous appearances in several movies and TV series, mostly on productions shot in Canada.
He is best known for his work in America on "24" as CTU Agent Curtis Manning, and in Canada for his work on "First Wave".
From 2012-2015, Cross appeared in the Canadian series "Continuum" as a main character.
He also appeared as Staff Sergeant Boyd Bloom in the Canadian police procedural "Motive", and as Six / Griffin Jones in the Syfy series "Dark Matter".
Jack Christie is a former Formula car racer and manager and adviser to drivers including Paul Tracy, Scott Goodyear, and Ron Fellows.
He founded the Canadian Formula 2000 series in 1981 and the Rothmans Porsche Challenge in 1986.

Canadian F2000 became the basis of all other F2000 series and Rothmans Porsche Challenge became the basis for the incredibly successful Porsche Supercup series around the world, although the Canadian series only lasted five years.
He was inducted into the Canadian Motorsport Hall of Fame in 2008.
The Cassinian is the latest age of the Canadian Epoch when thought of temporally and the uppermost stage of the Canadian Series when thought of stratigraphically.
The Canadian, either as a series or as an Epoch is the name that has been given to the Lower, or Early, Ordovician in North America and has been applied worldwide.
Daniel Gillies (born March 14, 1976) is a Canadian-born New Zealand actor.

He is best known for his role as Elijah Mikaelson on the television series "The Vampire Diaries" and its spin-off "The Originals", as well as Dr. Joel Goran on the Canadian series "Saving Hope", a medical themed show with a mystical, paranormal twist.
He wrote and directed the 2012 film "Broken Kingdom".
Dino Dan is a Canadian television series that was created and is directed by J. J. Johnson.
The series premiered on TVOKids in Canada on January 4, 2010 and on Nick Jr. in the United States on October 17, 2010.
The series also airs on Access, Knowledge Network, and SCN.
The series is produced by Sinking Ship Entertainment, in association with TVOKids, Access, Knowledge Network, and SCN.
A third season of the series, "Dino Dana", premiered on Amazon on May 26, 2017.
Dino Dana is about a nine-year-old girl who sets out to achieve "dino experiments" that teaches her more about the extinct animals.
Erika Tham (born 15 December 1998) is a Canadian actress, singer, and dancer.
She is known for her role as Corki Chang in the Canadian series "Make It Pop".

Lara Jean Chorostecki (born September 24, 1984) is a Canadian actress, known for her role as Fredricka "Freddie" Lounds in the American psychological thriller–horror television series "Hannibal", and as Sergeant Krystina Breeland on the critically acclaimed Canadian series "X Company".
Isaac Durnford (born February 26, 1998) is a Canadian actor, best known for his role as Cory Schluter in the Canadian TV series "Dino Dan".

candidate.summarize1.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `passages`, produce the field `summary`.

Role: This agent summarizes first-hop evidence and exposes the bridge entity, any missing fact(s), ambiguity, and concrete next-hop retrieval directions. Do not attempt to produce the final answer—the summary should tell the next agent exactly what to fetch to finish the multi-hop chain.

Output requirements (single `summary` value only):
- Start the summary with the header: First-hop findings:
- Include these labeled parts (use a short bullet list or 2–4 concise sentences; keep overall output brief):
  1) Evidence found: For each person/entity named in the question, list which passage(s) mention matching candidate pages/titles and quote any explicit fact tokens found there (names, birth years, publication years, short parenthetical notes). Keep quoted fragments <=15 words and cite the passage title/label for each fact.
  2) Ambiguities: If a name yields multiple candidate pages or variants in the passages, explicitly enumerate those candidate titles/labels as they appear; if an expected page/variant is missing, note that too.
  3) Bridge entity / Missing fact: Explicitly name the bridge entity exactly as it appears in the passages (the person, work, or organization that links to the next hop) and state the precise missing fact needed to answer the question (e.g., a birth date, founding year, canonical series ordering).
  4) Retrieval direction: Provide 2–4 concrete, prioritized next-hop targets (specific page titles or disambiguation pages to fetch, and/or exact sections to check such as lede/infobox/Career/History). Include short example search keywords or one-line instructions (e.g., "open '<Name>' page and check lede/infobox and 'Career' section for 'Italian champion' year").
- End with a brief explicit note that the final answer is deferred until those pages are retrieved.

Hard constraints (do not violate):
- Do not provide a final comparison or answer here. Do not guess or invent facts not present in the provided passages or introduce outside knowledge.
- Do not assume which candidate is intended when multiple candidates exist.
- Avoid long verbatim copying of passages (>15 words).
- Preserve concise factual phrasing and verbatim tokens that appear in the passages (names, dates, page titles) when reporting evidence.

Keep the summary concise and focused—just the first-hop findings needed to guide the next retrieval hop.
candidate.create_query_hop2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, produce the fields `query`.

Output contract:
- Produce exactly one field named `query` whose value is a single short retrieval query string (one line, no surrounding explanation or extra fields).
- Do not answer, summarize, or explain anything in `query`. Do not include fallback instructions or user-facing requests for more info in this field.

Construction rules (apply the most specific rule that fits the inputs):
1) Linking two named works: If `question` or `summary_1` names two or more specific works (songs, tracks, titles) and the missing evidence is the bridge between them, emit a concise interrogative that names both works and requests the linking evidence (artist / album / recording). Keep it short (about ≤ 12 words). Example form: "Which artist or album recorded both 'Work A' and 'Work B'?"
2) Missing album artist/genre: If `summary_1` indicates the album title is present but the artist or genre is unknown, emit a compact search string of the form: <AlbumTitle> album artist genre
   - No punctuation, no question phrasing, no extra words. Example: "Levitate album artist genre"
3) Sports season-year lookups: If the `question` contains a specific season-year token and the missing bridge is a league-season page, emit a short query that includes the year token, a league identifier (e.g., "NHL" or "National Hockey League"), and a cue such as "regular season" or "season count". Keep it concise (≤ ~10 tokens).
4) Default: Otherwise, produce one concise search-style query (≤ ~12 words) that uses visible tokens from `question` and `summary_1` targeted at retrieving the missing supporting evidence (artist, album, recording, genre, season page, etc.).

General guidance:
- Keep queries compact; avoid lists, long page titles, or copying large spans from hidden targets.
- Do not invent facts or candidate answers; use only visible tokens or neutral retrieval cues.
- Prefer a short search string; use an interrogative only for the two-works linking case in rule 1.
- The goal is to maximize recovery of the missing bridge evidence for the downstream retriever.

Return only the single `query` field.
candidate.summarize2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `context`, `passages`, produce the field `summary`.

Primary function: integrate the newly retrieved `passages` (second-hop evidence) with the provided `context` (first-hop) so the output is a grounded post-retrieval state that preserves the facts the final-answer generator needs. Resolve the earlier bridge when the supplied passages support doing so; retain uncertainty only when evidence is insufficient.

Behavior rules (minimal, general):
1) Preserve concise-answer behavior. If `context` already contains a concise direct answer (a short sentence or phrase), keep that text exactly as the first sentence of `summary`, unless it contradicts the `passages`.
2) When `passages` supply additional, directly supported facts about entities named in the `question` or `context`, append up to one brief supporting sentence per distinct named entity (keep total `summary` to 1–3 sentences, preferably 1–2). Each supporting sentence should: start with the entity's full name as shown in the passages, include a short paraphrased attribution drawn only from the passages (no more than ~10–12 words) such as nationality, a notable achievement, a birth date, or a current affiliation, and avoid inventing or inferring facts not present in the inputs.
3) When an object/entity page (for example, a car page, group page, or similar) lists associated people and a separate passage provides a biography for one of those listed people, produce a 1–2 sentence integrated summary: (a) first sentence: name the people as listed on the object page (e.g., "driven by X and Y" or "member of Group Z"); (b) optional second short clause/sentence: paraphrase key biographical facts from the person's passage (only facts present there — e.g., nationality, notable wins/awards, birth date, current team/affiliation). Keep wording compact and factual.
4) Ground everything in the provided `passages` and `context`. Do not add new claims, dates, roles, or affiliations not explicitly supported. Avoid long verbatim quotations from the passages; you may paraphrase short phrases (≤10 words) only if they appear in the sources, and you may copy short strings such as names or dates exactly as shown.
5) If the passages do not provide enough evidence to resolve the bridge, state that the passages do not specify the requested fact while still listing any supported names or facts. Do not guess or fabricate missing information.
6) Output contract: return only the field `summary` containing 1–3 concise sentences (preferably 1–2) that integrate the first-hop `context` and second-hop `passages` according to the rules above. Do not add commentary, answer-labels, or extraneous text.
candidate.final_answer.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, and `summary_2`, produce the field `answer`.

Instructions:
- Use only the visible summaries (summary_1 and summary_2) together with the question as your evidence. Do not introduce external facts, sources, or claims not present in those summaries.
- Treat summary_2 (the later, integrated post-retrieval state) as primary evidence while still considering summary_1. Do not assume the final state is unresolved simply because summary_1 describes an earlier unresolved bridge; rely on the combined visible summaries to reach a conclusion.
- If summary_1 and summary_2 both clearly and unambiguously support the same binary (yes/no) conclusion to the question, then the value of the `answer` field must be exactly one lowercase token: either "yes" (if both summaries affirm the fact) or "no" (if both summaries deny the fact). The `answer` field must contain only that single token and nothing else (no punctuation, no capitalization, no explanation, no additional words).
- If the summaries conflict, are ambiguous, or do not together provide a clear binary yes/no verdict, produce a concise answer string in the `answer` field that best reflects what the summaries support. This concise answer may be a short phrase or sentence, but must still be supported solely by the summaries and must avoid adding new facts.
- Always minimize verbosity: prefer the single-token binary response when the summaries unambiguously agree; otherwise keep the answer brief and directly tied to the visible summaries.

Produce only the `answer` field as output.

response.hop1_query: Isaac Durnford had a role on the Canadian series directed by whom?
hop1_docs: Isaac Durnford | Isaac Durnford (born February 26, 1998) is a Canadian actor, best known for his role as Cory Schluter in the Canadian TV series "Dino Dan".

Tabitha Lupien | Tabitha Lupien (born c. 1988) is a Canadian actress and competitive dancer trained in ballet, tap, jazz, pointe, hip hop, and acrobatics.  She trains with her sisters Lindsay and Samantha and her brother Isaac at the Canadian Dance Company, owned by her parents Allain and Dawn, located in Oakville, Ontario.  She is best known for her role as Julie Ubriacco from "Look Who's Talking Now".  She had a minor role in the 2007 film version of "Hairspray" as Becky and guest starred in TV series The L.A.Complex as a defeated dancer 6 years later

Durnford School | Durnford School was a notoriously spartan and uncomfortable preparatory school which opened in 1894 on the Isle of Purbeck in Dorset.  The school occupied Durnford House, in High Street (postcode BH19 3HB) in the village of Langton Matravers near Swanage.  "Strip and swim" was the morning ritual for the boys – watched by headmaster Thomas Pellatt – into the sea from Dancing Ledge on the coast in 1898.  Later, Pellatt had quarrymen blast out a pool in the rocks of Dancing Ledge for his pupils to swim.  Pellatt wrote his reminiscences in a book: "Boys in the making" 1936.

Mount Durnford | Mount Durnford ( ) is a mountain, 2,715 m high, standing 5 nmi southeast of Mount Field in the Churchill Mountains of Antarctica.  It was discovered and named "Durnford Bluff" by the British National Antarctic Expedition, 1901–04, for Admiral Sir John Durnford, a Junior Naval Lord who was of assistance to the expedition.  The New Zealand Geological Survey Antarctic Expedition (1960–61) remapped the feature and amended the name to Mount Durnford.

Isaac Hempstead Wright | Isaac Hempstead-Wright (born Isaac William Hempstead; 9 April 1999) is an English actor.  He is best known for his role as Bran Stark on the HBO series "Game of Thrones", which earned him a Young Artist Award nomination as Best Young Supporting Actor in a TV Series.

The Hayseeds | The Hayseeds is a 1933 Australian musical comedy from Beaumont Smith.  It centres on the rural family, the Hayseeds, about whom Smith had previously made six silent films, starting with "Our Friends, the Hayseeds" (1917).  He retired from directing in 1925 but decided to revive the series in the wake of the box office success of "On Our Selection" (1932).  It was the first starring role in a movie for stage actor Cecil Kellaway.

Veedu Oru Swargam | Veedu Oru Swargam is a 1977 Indian Malayalam film, directed by Jesey and produced by Isaac John.  The film stars Prem Nazir, Sheela, Sukumari and KPAC Lalitha in lead roles.  The film had musical score by G. Devarajan.
response.summary_1:
First-hop findings:
- Evidence found: Isaac Durnford — passage [1] states he is "born February 26, 1998" and "best known for his role as Cory Schluter in the Canadian TV series 'Dino Dan'." (cite: [1]).
- Ambiguities: Only one series title appears in the passages: "Dino Dan". No alternate series pages or disambiguation for "Dino Dan" are provided in the passages.
- Bridge entity / Missing fact: Bridge entity = "Dino Dan" (as shown in passage [1]). Missing fact needed = the name of the director of the Canadian series "Dino Dan".
- Retrieval direction (prioritized):
  1) Open the "Dino Dan" page (page lede and infobox) and check for a "Directed by" or "Credits/Production" field. Search keywords: "Dino Dan director", "Dino Dan (TV series) directed by".
  2) If needed, open the "Dino Dan" episode list or individual episode pages to check episode-level directors/series creators and credits.
  3) Check the Isaac Durnford page's Filmography/Credits section for a link to "Dino Dan" that may include director/production credits.
Final answer deferred until those pages/sections are retrieved.
hop2_docs: List of Dino Dan episodes | "Dino Dan" is a Canadian television series that was created and is directed by J. J. Johnson.  The series premiered on TVOKids in Canada on January 4, 2010 and on Nick Jr. in the United States on October 17, 2010.  A third season of the series, "Dino Dan", has been aired on TVOKids in Canada and instead of being aired on Nick Jr. in the United States, it is streamed on Amazon.com in the United States.

Dino Dan | Dino Dan is a Canadian television series that was created and is directed by J. J. Johnson.  The series premiered on TVOKids in Canada on January 4, 2010 and on Nick Jr. in the United States on October 17, 2010.  The series also airs on Access, Knowledge Network, and SCN.  The series is produced by Sinking Ship Entertainment, in association with TVOKids, Access, Knowledge Network, and SCN.  A third season of the series, "Dino Dana", premiered on Amazon on May 26, 2017.  Dino Dana is about a nine-year-old girl who sets out to achieve "dino experiments" that teaches her more about the extinct animals.

Dinos Dimopoulos | Dinos Dimopoulos (Greek: Ντίνος Δημόπουλος ; 22 August 1921 – 28 February 2003) was a Greek actor, film director, screenwriter and theatre director.  He directed 47 films between 1953 and 1993.
Dino Falconi | Dino Falconi (1902–1990) was an Italian screenwriter and film director.  The actor Arturo Falconi was his uncle.

Dinos Katsouridis | Dinos Katsouridis (1927 - 28 November 2011) was a Greek Cypriot film director, cinematographer and editor.  He had worked in a lot of Greek film sometimes as director, sometimes as film editor and sometimes as cinematographer as well as writer.  He has won film awards about all the categories of these works
Isaac Durnford | Isaac Durnford (born February 26, 1998) is a Canadian actor, best known for his role as Cory Schluter in the Canadian TV series "Dino Dan".

Dino Boffo | Dino Boffo (born 19 August 1952) is an Italian journalist.  From 1994 to 2009, he was editor of the newspaper "Avvenire", and from, 2010 to 2014 director of the television network TV2000 .
response.summary_2: Isaac Durnford (born February 26, 1998) is best known for his role as Cory Schluter in the Canadian TV series "Dino Dan". J. J. Johnson is credited as the creator and director of "Dino Dan".
